description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular brick structure with a prominent hip roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The front features a large open bay supported by wooden posts, providing access for livestock and ventilation. The brick walls are solid, with a single rectangular window visible on one side and an open section above the main entrance. The roof extends well beyond the walls, creating a sheltered overhang around the perimeter. The interior appears partially open, with wooden beams and slats visible through the front and side openings. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing durability, ventilation, and ease of access for agricultural use.
description: A brick cowshed with a hip shingle roof, open front bay, and wooden support posts, designed for livestock shelter and ventilation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large open bay, wooden posts, and partial interior with wooden beams.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the deep roof overhang, open slatted section, and solid brick wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a fully enclosed brick wall and the expanse of the hip roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a single rectangular window and visible roof overhang.
attribute_door_style: open bay entrance with wooden support posts, no visible hinged or sliding doors
attribute_window_presence: one rectangular window on the side wall
attribute_ventilation_features: open bay entrance, open slatted section above entrance
